frame.description: How you frame the problem: the decomposition, the variables that actually move the decision, the lens you are reasoning through. 2-5 sentences.

evidence_gathered.description: Facts you gathered with your own tools. An entry is GROUNDED only with re-openable provenance. Asserting you found something is NOT evidence.

locator.description: Re-openable provenance: file path + line range (e.g. src/x.ts:40-58), a full URL, or a verbatim quoted excerpt. Empty string if you have none.

retrieval_query.description: The exact search query or how it was located. Empty string if N/A (e.g. theory).

grounded.description: true ONLY if locator is a real, re-openable path/URL/quote. theory and prior_knowledge are never grounded.

assumptions.description: The assumptions your framing rests on — candidate cruxes.

load_bearing_because.description: Why the recommendation would change if this were false.

risks.description: R1-Critic beat: the strongest challenges to the problem/proposal AS POSED, and its real risks. Genuine, not performative.

reframe.description: null unless there is a MATERIALLY stronger framing backed by a concrete crux. High bar — default to null for a well-posed question. Never second-guess for its own sake.

proposed_question.description: The question that may be the real decision.

crux.description: Why Y may be the real question: the shaky assumption Z behind X, and how to tell which frame is right.

capability_gaps_hit.description: Facts or tools you needed but could not get. Material only — do not pad. Each becomes an unverified crux or a remedy note.

want.description: The fact/tool you needed but lacked.

remedy.description: e.g. 'add a web-search model' (missing_tool) or 'provide your real p99 latency' (missing_user_info).
